key_insight: The paper reframes structured preconditioning as a geometric distance-and-reachability problem: how far a restricted metric family must move to achieve a target condition-number class.
engineering_relevance: It gives a principled way to certify whether diagonal, block, or Kronecker preconditioners can actually deliver the desired conditioning under structural constraints.
method: Defines restricted dynamic geometric complexity as an intrinsic certificate distance.
Proves monotonicity and submanifold-distance principles for admissible metric families.
Derives LMI feasibility tests, exact 2D diagonal complexity, and Kronecker-based reachability/certificate conditions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports deterministic toy and synthetic workflows that verify diagonal, block, and Kronecker certificates on small quadratic instances, along with low-rank spectral monotonicity checks.
watch_item: Several low-rank, stochastic, and discretized quantities are presented mainly as diagnostic interfaces, so practical performance may depend on solver quality and the specific metric family chosen.
paper_title: Restricted Dynamic Geometric Complexity: Certificates for Structured Preconditioning
